data.title: Realty Income partners with GIC to develop industrial sites in U.S. and Mexico
data.description:
Realty Income (O) and global investment firm GIC, under the partnership, will develop industrial properties in the U.S., GIC will invest in Realty Income's U.S. Core Plus fund, and Realty Income (O) will commit to $200M to a Mexico build-to-suit program, bringing the REIT into Mexico, the companies said on Monday.

The relationship is expected to be long-term and programmatic in nature and is a component of Realty Income's private capital initiative aimed at diversifying its sources of capital beyond the public capital markets, while expanding its investable universe to core investment opportunities with attractive potential long-term return profiles, the company said. Furthermore, it builds on GIC's conviction that the net lease sector will produce strong, predictable cash yields.

"This partnership complements our existing direct exposure to the U.S. net lease space, unlocking direct access to the logistics investment-grade segment of the market where we expect continued growth," said Cai Wenzheng, head of Americas, real estate, at GIC.

The joint venture will focus on build-to-suit development of high-quality logistics real estate pre-leased under long-term net leases to tenants with investment grade-equivalent credit profiles in the U.S. It will have more than $1.5B of combined capital commitments from the two partners.

Also, as part of the partnership, Realty Income (O) and GIC will jointly finance the construction of industrial properties in Mexico, with Realty Income agreeing to purchase the properties upon completion of construction for a combined purchase price of approximately $200M. These logistics assets are located in Mexico City and Guadalajara and are pre-leased to Global Fortune 100 companies under long term net lease agreements.

GIC will be a cornerstone investor in Realty Income's (O) U.S. Core Plus fund, the companies said.

"Our long-term capital, paired with Realty Income's expertise and market access will allow us to unlock significant value through investments in mission-critical, state-of-the-art build-to-suit logistics asset," said, Goh Chin Kiong, CIC's chief investment officer of real estate.

Realty Income (O) stock was little changed in premarket trading.

data.title: Vir Biotechnology reports positive Phase 2 hepatitis delta data, outlines 2026 clinical milestones
data.description:
• Vir Biotechnology (VIR) on Monday reported updated Phase 2 data showing its combination therapy of tobevibart and elebsiran achieved undetectable hepatitis delta virus RNA in 88% of evaluable patients at 96 weeks of treatment, as the company outlined program updates and clinical milestones for 2026.
  • The company said the combination was well tolerated in the ongoing SOLSTICE trial, with no grade 3 or higher treatment-related adverse events reported in the combination arm. The data will be presented at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.
  • Vir said its registrational Phase 3 ECLIPSE program in chronic hepatitis delta is ongoing, with topline data from the ECLIPSE 1 trial expected in the fourth quarter of 2026 and data from ECLIPSE 2 and 3 expected in the first quarter of 2027.
  • In oncology, Vir said new Phase 1 safety and efficacy data for VIR-5500, its PSMA-targeted dual-masked T-cell engager for prostate cancer, will be presented at the ASCO Genitourinary Cancers Symposium in February 2026.
  • The company reported year-end 2025 cash, cash equivalents and investments of about $781 million and said its cash runway extends into the fourth quarter of 2027, following a licensing agreement with Norgine and continued cost discipline.
  • VIR closed -1.68% at $5.85.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lexicon flags $10 mln Novo Nordisk milestone and 2026 NDA plans
data.description:
• Lexicon Pharmaceuticals (LXRX) on Monday said its late-stage SONATA-HCM study of sotagliflozin remains on track to complete enrollment in 2026, with topline data expected in early 2027, as the company outlined business and pipeline updates at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.
  • The drugmaker said it triggered a $10 million milestone payment in January under its licensing agreement with Novo Nordisk related to obesity drug candidate LX9851, and noted the potential for up to an additional $20 million in milestones in 2026.
  • Lexicon also said it remains on track to resubmit a New Drug Application for Zynquista, its sotagliflozin-based treatment for type 1 diabetes, in 2026, following feedback from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and additional data from the STENO1 study.
  • For hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, the company said the Phase 3 SONATA-HCM trial is targeting enrollment of 500 patients, with completion expected in mid-2026.
  • Lexicon reported ending 2025 with $125.2 million in cash, investments and restricted cash, which it said is sufficient to fund operations into 2027.
  • LXRX -2.56% premarket to $1.14.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Butterfly Network expects at least 17% Q4 revenue growth
data.description:
• Butterfly Network (BFLY) expects to report Q4 2025 revenue growth of at least 17% Y/Y.
  • The company cited strong momentum in its handheld ultrasound business entering 2026.
  • Management highlighted 2026 as a potential inflection year driven by scaling its core commercial business and expanding Ultrasound-on-Chip co-development and licensing opportunities.

data.title: EDAP TMS reports Q4, FY HIFU revenue; issues 2026 guidance
data.description:
• EDAP TMS (EDAP) reported preliminary worldwide HIFU revenue of ~$12.9M–$13.3M in Q4 2025 and $36.7M–$37.2M for full-year 2025, the highest in company history.
  • Performance was driven by a record 15 Focal One system placements in Q4.
  • The company issued initial 2026 guidance, projecting core HIFU revenue of $50M–$54M and non-core revenue of $22M–$26M.

data.title: A new era of airline consolidation may have kicked off with Allegiant buying Sun Country
data.description:
The U.S. airline sector is on watch after Allegiant Travel (ALGT) struck a deal to acquire fellow budget carrier Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) in a cash-and-stock deal valued at about $1.5 billion, including roughly $400 million of Sun Country (SNCY) debt. Sun Country shareholders will receive $4.10 in cash plus 0.1557 Allegiant shares per share, implying a price of $18.89, about a 20% premium. The combined leisure-focused airline, operating under the Allegiant name, will serve more than 650 routes and nearly 200 aircraft, with headquarters in Las Vegas and a major presence in Minneapolis–St. Paul.

Notably, the combined company expects to realize revenue and cost synergies from scale efficiencies, fleet optimization, and procurement. 

Jefferies analyst Sheila Kahyaoglu noted the merger creates the 9th largest U.S. airline by domestic capacity at around 2.6% of the market, still well below the market shares of American Airlines Group (AAL) at 21%, Delta Air Lines (DAL) at 19%, United Airlines (UAL) at 19%, and Southwest Airlines (LUV) at 18%. Importantly, Kahyaoglu said the deal marks the return of U.S. airline consolidation, which is considered a major topic for investors in 2026 and beyond.

Bank of America analyst Andrew Didora said the firm views the transaction as an industry positive, assuming it completes, as it could remove two potential bidders for Spirit’s assets. That is seen as increasing the likelihood that more capacity comes out of the market in the event Spirit does not emerge from bankruptcy. "Further, in our view, consolidation is a positive, particularly for an industry segment that has struggled to drive growth/margins," wrote Didora. 

Shares of Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) were up 13.1% in premarket trading, while Allegiant Travel (ALGT) fell 2.1%.

Airline stocks: American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV), United Airlines (UAL), JetBlue Airways (JBLU), Alaska Air Group (ALK), Allegiant Travel (ALGT), Spirit Airlines (SAVEQ) (FLYYQ), Mesa Airlines (MESA), SkyWest (SKYW), Sun Country Airlines (SNCY), Republic Airways (RJET), and Frontier Group (ULCC).

data.title: GeneDx falls after reporting Q4 and FY25 revenue
data.description:
• GeneDx reported full-year 2025 revenue of approximately $427M, inline with consensus of $427M, representing a 41% year-over-year increase and within its previously raised guidance range of $425 million to $428 million.

  • Exome and genome revenue was approximately $360M, reflecting 54% year-over-year growth (58% excluding a one-time 2024 benefit), in line with prior guidance of 53% to 55% growth.

  • Exome and genome test result volume totaled 97,271 for the year, an increase of 30.5% year over year, exceeding the company’s prior guidance of at least 30% growth.

  • Fourth-quarter 2025 revenue was approximately $121M, slightly higher than consensus of $120.5M, up 27% year over year.

  •  

    Exome and genome revenue in the quarter was approximately $104M, representing 32% year-over-year growth (43% excluding a one-time 2024 benefit).

     

  • Exome and genome test result volume reached 27,761 in the quarter, an increase of 34.3% year over year.

  • Full-year and fourth-quarter 2024 revenues and adjusted gross margin included a previously reported $6.8 million discrete benefit related to a multi-year appeal recovery from a single third-party payer. This benefit consisted of $5.8 million attributed to exome and genome revenue and $1.0 million attributed to other test lines in the fourth quarter of 2024.

  • Cash, cash equivalents, marketable securities, and restricted cash totaled approximately $172 million as of December 31, 2025.

  • The company issued full-year 2026 guidance expecting revenue between $540 million and $555 million ($531M consensus), including 33% to 35% growth in exome and genome revenue. Adjusted net income is expected to be positive.

  • Ms. Stueland will present at the 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on January 14.

  • WGS -11.3% premarket to $119.99

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chesapeake Gold launches $10M bought deal public offering
data.description:
• Chesapeake Gold (CKG:CA) has entered into an agreement with Red Cloud Securities as lead underwriter and sole bookrunner, pursuant to which underwriters have agreed to purchase for resale 2.38M units of the company at a price of $4.20 per unit on a "bought deal" basis for gross proceeds of $10M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one common share at a price of $5.65 at any time on or before that date which is 36 months following the closing date.
  • The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to advance the company's proprietary oxidative leach technology, the Metates project, the Lucy project and for general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dexcom reports 13% rise in preliminary Q4 revenue, issues 2026 outlook
data.description:
• DexCom (DXCM) on Monday reported preliminary, unaudited results for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025 with total revenue of approximately $1.26 billion, up 13% from a year earlier. 
  • U.S. revenue is expected to be about $892 million, up 11%, while international revenue is seen roughly at $368 million, an increase of 18%, the company said.
  • For fiscal 2025, total preliminary, unaudited revenue is approximately $4.662 billion, up 16% from 2024 and reiterated its non-GAAP gross margin guidance of about 61% and operating margin guidance of 20% to 21%.
  • Looking ahead, the continuous glucose monitoring maker forecast 2026 revenue of $5.16 billion to $5.25 billion, implying growth of about 11% to 13%. It also expects non-GAAP gross margins of about 63% to 64% and operating margins of 22% to 23% for the year.
  • Dexcom will report its audited full fourth quarter and fiscal 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 12, 2026 after the close of market, followed by a conference call with management.
  • DXCM -0.0% premarket to $67.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Axsome Therapeutics announces prelim Q4, FY revenue above estimates
data.description:
• Axsome Therapeutics (AXSM) announced preliminary net product revenue of ~$196M for Q4 2025, representing 65% Y/Y growth, compared to estimate of $187M.
  • Full-year 2025 net product revenue is expected to be ~$638.5M, reflecting 66% growth Y/Y, vs estimate of $629.5M.
  • Revenue growth was driven by sales of AUVELITY, SUNOSI, and SYMBRAVO.
